inline_r8_source_object.role: The wrapper R8 input that later becomes constructor field +0x30 is an inline local string object at [rbp -0x70], built immediately before the wrapper call by a range-style string helper rather than forwarded as an opaque pointer.

evidence.observation: loads RCX = [rbp -0x70], proving the later wrapper R8 register points at an inline local object.
calls helper 0x140031970 to populate that object.

helper 0x140031970 preserves or allocates destination string storage, copies an incoming source slice with 0x1407B9C90, writes a trailing NUL, and records the resulting length/capacity metadata on the destination object.
constructor 0x14058EE40 copies wrapper R8 into persistent field +0x30 via 0x140032150, tying the caller-built local object directly to the retained JS5/bootstrap object.

careful_inference.statement: The exact semantic label of the [rbp -0x70] string is still unresolved, but it is no longer just an unnamed 'context object'; it is a concrete string object assembled from a selected source slice before the two later endpoint-format stages.

post_constructor_payload_builder.role: After the main constructor fields are installed, a second stage builds a refcounted payload object at [rsi +0x18] / [rsi +0x20] and serializes a typed, three-string payload plus trailing scalar fields into its buffer.

evidence.observation: allocates a new refcounted object, allocates backing storage sized from three incoming string lengths, and stores the new object and its buffer view into [rsi +0x18] and [rsi +0x20].
writes leading byte 0x02 into the buffer and advances the write cursor.
calls 0x14002BFA0 three times with the buffer builder and three different string objects.

helper 0x14002BFA0 resolves string-object storage, copies the string bytes into [builder +0x10/+0x18], advances the builder cursor, and writes a trailing NUL, making it a concrete append-string-into-buffer helper.
appends one extra byte from the object at [rsi +0x10148] +0x20.
appends a trailing 16-bit value sourced from [arg7 +0x8], with a byte-swap path when the global endianness sentinel at 0x140B2708C matches 0x3020100.

string_inputs.meaning: constructor register R9: the second %s:%u string clone from wrapper-local [rsp +0x70]
constructor arg5: the first formatter-backed text clone from wrapper-local [rsp +0x88]
constructor arg13: the third text clone sourced from wrapper-local [rsp +0xA0]

careful_inference.statement: This payload is very likely a typed bootstrap descriptor or follow-on message rather than a cosmetic string bundle, because it is length-sized up front, receives a type byte, appends three concrete text fields, and then adds trailing scalar data before being retained on the persistent JS5/bootstrap object.

secondary_sink.role: A separate subordinate object at [rsi +0x10] receives a literal-backed callback/update before the three-string payload is built.

evidence.observation: builds a small literal-backed stack descriptor rooted at 0x140B99238 and dispatches it through virtual slot +0x20 on [rsi +0x10] +0x1E8.

reconciled_conclusions: The wrapper R8 input that becomes constructor field +0x30 is an inline caller-built string object, not a generic context handle.

The three wrapper-local text clones are used in two distinct downstream ways: an earlier shared-callback publication path through 0x140E57B40 and a later persistent payload builder rooted at [rsi +0x18]/[rsi +0x20].
Helper 0x14002BFA0 is now a concrete string-append helper for that persistent payload builder, which materially tightens the JS5/bootstrap serialization model.
impact_on_prior_artifacts: Strengthens win64c-js5-wrapper-argument-normalization-and-constructor-field-map by replacing the vague '+0x30 context object' wording with a concrete caller-built inline string object.
Extends the post-construction model beyond the 0x140E57B40 callback path: the three cloned strings also feed a separate retained payload object on the persistent JS5/bootstrap structure.
next_targets: Name the semantic source of the string slice copied into [rbp -0x70] before helper 0x140031970.
Classify the persistent payload object at [rsi +0x18]/[rsi +0x20] by following later consumers instead of only its local serializer.
Name the global/config object at 0x140C57478 now that it is proven to feed constructor field +0x48 and the later payload tail.
